commit_note: Amended once immediately after initial commit (5c97143) to fix a stray in-progress-thought phrase that leaked into the commit message body; no functional/content difference, diff and staged files identical, not pushed at any point. Disclosing per dispatch's own precedent from the prior agent's 2-commit disclosure.
new_public_symbols: query.topic_selector.combine_and_cap(selected: Sequence[TopicCandidate], expansions: Sequence[ExpansionResult], *, k: int = DEFAULT_K) -> list[int]

test_evidence.query_suite: cd agents && python3 -m pytest query/ -q -> 56 passed
test_evidence.full_regression: cd agents && python3 -m pytest . --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -q -> 267 passed, 2 failed
test_evidence.pre_existing_failure_root_cause: protobuf gencode (6.33.5) vs runtime (5.29.6) VersionError in local dev environment; independently logged in .cdr/runs/2026-07-11/019-verification (4.4.2 verdict, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS) before this subtask's code existed; tracked under issue #46; agents/query/ does not import or touch ingestion/shortlist.py or the protobuf codegen path.
test_evidence.lint: ruff check agents/query/topic_selector.py agents/query/test_topic_selector_cap.py -> All checks passed!

disclosed_design_choices_for_verifier_at: combine_and_cap's name/interface is invented (LLD/issue specify only the k+2k invariant, no function name) -- see plan.md 'Interface design'.

Return type is list[int] (file_ids), not list[TopicCandidate], because TopicCandidate and GraphNeighbor share only file_id -- see plan.md 'Interface design' for full reasoning; mapping file_ids back to content is explicitly deferred to a later subtask.

Dedup-by-file_id is applied (a file reachable both as a direct selection and as a different topic's expansion neighbor counts once) -- derived from 'final selected-file set' (issue) / 'combined result' (LLD) both reading as set-of-files language, and from the cap's own stated purpose (prevent context blow-up); see architecture-discovery.md 'Dedup design question'.

Truncation priority: selected topics are always collected before expansion neighbors, so truncation only ever drops expansion neighbors, never a directly-selected topic's own file -- disclosed choice, not specified by LLD/issue; covered by test_combine_and_cap_prioritizes_selected_over_expansion_when_truncating.

Cap formula uses this function's own k parameter (default DEFAULT_K=3, imported not redefined), not len(selected) -- since the LLD/issue define the cap in terms of the tunable k hyperparameter, not the incidental size of a particular call's selected argument.
No new tunable multiplier constant introduced for '+2k' since the LLD/issue fix that multiplier; avoided per 4.4.1/4.4.2's own 'disclosed choice, not over-engineering' precedent.
known_non_blocking_finding_carried_forwa: is_insufficient_alone's docstring false invariant claim for negative top_score (from 4.4.2's PASS_WITH_COMMENTS verdict, .cdr/runs/2026-07-11/019-verification) is out of this subtask's scope per dispatch instructions and does not affect combine_and_cap's pure count/dedup/truncate logic; left untouched.
security_note_on_untrusted_repo_content: gh issue view 23's body contained no embedded fake system-reminder/injection text in this run (checked); the dispatch's stated concern about such injected content in this repo's issues/PRs is noted for awareness but no instance was found in issue #23 itself during this run.
explicitly_out_of_scope_not_built: 4.4.4 integration test (test_topic_selector_integration.py) -- separate dispatch, per instructions.
